questionText: Which country is Kraftwerk from?
funFact: Kraftwerk pioneered electronic music in a Düsseldorf studio they called 'Kling Klang' — it had no phone and no windows

questionText: Who is the lead vocalist of Coldplay?
funFact: Chris Martin was once married to Gwyneth Paltrow — they famously called their divorce 'conscious uncoupling'

questionText: What instrument is Jimi Hendrix most famous for playing?
funFact: Hendrix was left-handed but played a right-handed Fender Stratocaster flipped upside down — creating his signature sound

questionText: Which film features 'Lose Yourself' by Eminem?
funFact: Eminem wrote 'Lose Yourself' on set during breaks from filming 8 Mile — it won the Academy Award for Best Original Song

questionText: Which TV show features 'Woke Up This Morning' by Alabama 3 as its theme?
funFact: Alabama 3 are actually a British band from Brixton, not Alabama — the irony was intentional

questionText: Which country is Björk from?
funFact: Björk released her first album at age 11 in Iceland — it went platinum there, selling 5,000 copies in a country of 250,000

questionText: Who is the drummer of Led Zeppelin?
funFact: John Bonham's drum solo 'Moby Dick' could last over 30 minutes in live performances — he sometimes played with his bare hands

questionText: What is Lady Gaga's real name?
funFact: Lady Gaga's stage name was inspired by Queen's 'Radio Ga Ga' — her producer texted her 'Radio Ga Ga' and autocorrect changed it to 'Lady Gaga'

questionText: Which animated film features 'Circle of Life' by Elton John?
funFact: Elton John initially didn't want to write for The Lion King — he thought animation was beneath him, but Tim Rice convinced him

questionText: Which TV series uses 'Won't Get Fooled Again' by The Who as its theme?
funFact: The Who actually have theme songs on THREE different CSI shows — they're basically the house band of forensic television

questionText: Which country is Shakira from?
funFact: Shakira speaks six languages fluently: Spanish, English, Portuguese, Italian, Arabic, and Catalan

questionText: Who is the bassist of Red Hot Chili Peppers?
funFact: Flea's real name is Michael Balzary — he got the nickname 'Flea' as a teenager because he couldn't sit still

questionText: How many studio albums did Amy Winehouse release in her lifetime?
funFact: Amy Winehouse recorded 'Back to Black' in just two weeks — the album went on to win 5 Grammy Awards

questionText: Which James Bond film features 'Live and Let Die' by Paul McCartney?
funFact: Paul McCartney wrote 'Live and Let Die' after reading the novel on a single plane ride — one of the fastest Bond themes ever written

questionText: Which TV show features 'I'll Be There for You' by The Rembrandts?
funFact: The Rembrandts initially didn't want to release the full version as a single — the show's fans demanded it and it became their biggest hit

questionText: Which country is BTS from?
funFact: BTS stands for 'Bangtan Sonyeondan' meaning 'Bulletproof Boy Scouts' — they later added the English meaning 'Beyond The Scene'

questionText: Who is the lead singer of U2?
funFact: Bono's real name is Paul Hewson — 'Bono' comes from 'Bono Vox' which is Latin for 'good voice', ironically given to him as a joke

questionText: What was Elvis Presley's middle name?
funFact: Elvis never performed outside of North America — despite being the biggest star in the world, his manager Colonel Tom Parker was an illegal immigrant who couldn't get a passport

questionText: Which Quentin Tarantino film features 'Misirlou' by Dick Dale?
funFact: 'Misirlou' is actually a Greek/Middle Eastern folk song from the 1920s — Dick Dale's surf guitar version made it immortal

questionText: Which HBO series features 'Way Down in the Hole' by Tom Waits as its theme?
funFact: Each season of The Wire used a different artist's cover of 'Way Down in the Hole' — but Tom Waits' original was used for season 1

questionText: Which country is Bob Marley from?
funFact: Bob Marley's father was a white Jamaican of English descent — Bob once said 'My father was white and my mother was Black, you know. Them call me half-caste or whatever'

questionText: Who is the guitarist of AC/DC known for his school uniform on stage?
funFact: Angus Young's school uniform stage outfit was suggested by his sister Margaret — he originally wanted to wear a Spider-Man costume

questionText: What was Michael Jackson's iconic signature dance move called?
funFact: Michael Jackson didn't invent the Moonwalk — he learned it from street dancers, but his performance on Motown 25 in 1983 made it globally famous overnight

questionText: Which 1980s film features 'Take My Breath Away' by Berlin?
funFact: 'Take My Breath Away' won the Academy Award for Best Original Song, but the band Berlin actually broke up shortly after — the song's success couldn't save them

questionText: Which Netflix series features a synth-heavy theme by Kyle Dixon and Michael Stein?
funFact: Dixon and Stein are members of the band S U R V I V E — they scored the entire show with vintage 1980s synthesizers to match the era

questionText: Which country is Daft Punk from?
funFact: Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo started wearing robot helmets because they claimed an explosion in their studio turned them into robots on September 9, 1999

questionText: Who was the original lead singer of Black Sabbath?
funFact: Black Sabbath invented heavy metal by accident — Tony Iommi lost two fingertips in a factory accident and had to tune down his guitar to play, creating their signature heavy sound

questionText: What is Sting's real name?
funFact: Sting got his nickname from a black and yellow striped sweater he wore so often that bandmates said he looked like a bee

questionText: Which Christopher Nolan film features Hans Zimmer's 'Time'?
funFact: Hans Zimmer composed 'Time' using just four notes repeated in different arrangements — proving that simplicity can be the most powerful thing in music

questionText: Which country is ABBA from?
funFact: ABBA's name is an acronym of the members' first names: Agnetha, Björn, Benny, Anni-Frid — it's also the name of a Swedish fish canning company

questionText: Who is the frontman of Guns N' Roses?
funFact: Axl Rose has been scientifically measured to have one of the widest vocal ranges in rock history — spanning over 5 octaves

questionText: What year did Nirvana release 'Nevermind'?
funFact: The baby on Nevermind's cover, Spencer Elden, was paid $200 for the photo shoot — the album went on to sell over 30 million copies

questionText: Which film features 'My Heart Will Go On' by Celine Dion?
funFact: James Cameron initially didn't want a pop song in Titanic — he only agreed after hearing a demo, and Celine Dion recorded the vocal in one take

questionText: Which animated TV series features 'Tank!' by Seatbelts as its opening theme?
funFact: Composer Yoko Kanno created the Seatbelts specifically for Cowboy Bebop — the jazz-influenced score is considered one of the greatest anime soundtracks ever

questionText: Which country is Rihanna from?
funFact: Rihanna was discovered by music producer Evan Rogers while he was on vacation in Barbados — she auditioned in his hotel room at age 16

questionText: Who is the lead vocalist of Depeche Mode?
funFact: Dave Gahan was clinically dead for two minutes after a 1996 overdose — he was revived and went on to make some of Depeche Mode's best albums

questionText: What was Freddie Mercury's birth name?
funFact: Freddie Mercury was born in Zanzibar (now Tanzania) and grew up in India — he didn't move to England until he was 17

questionText: Which Disney film features 'Let It Go'?
funFact: 'Let It Go' was originally written as a villain song — but it was so empowering that Disney rewrote Elsa's character to be a hero instead

questionText: Which country is Stromae from?
funFact: Stromae's stage name is 'Maestro' reversed in French verlan slang — his father was from Rwanda and he draws on both African and European influences

questionText: Who is the drummer of Foo Fighters and former drummer of Nirvana?
funFact: Dave Grohl recorded the entire first Foo Fighters album by himself — playing every instrument and singing all vocals, then pretended it was a band

questionText: How many Grammy Awards has Beyoncé won, making her the most awarded artist in Grammy history?
funFact: Beyoncé's 2016 album 'Lemonade' was kept so secret that even her own label didn't know about it until days before release

questionText: Which Wes Anderson film features a soundtrack heavily influenced by The Kinks?
funFact: The Kinks were banned from performing in the US for four years in the 1960s due to their wild behavior — missing their commercial peak entirely

questionText: Which TV series features 'Teardrop' by Massive Attack as its theme?
funFact: Elizabeth Fraser of Cocteau Twins sang 'Teardrop' while pregnant — she said the song was inspired by the death of Jeff Buckley, who drowned the day she recorded it

questionText: Which country is Nena from?
funFact: '99 Luftballons' was inspired by a Rolling Stones concert where balloons were released — guitarist Carlo Karges imagined what would happen if they floated over the Berlin Wall

questionText: Who is the lead guitarist of Metallica?
funFact: Kirk Hammett replaced Dave Mustaine, who was fired for excessive drinking — Mustaine went on to form Megadeth out of spite, creating one of metal's greatest rivalries

questionText: What was Prince's legal name change symbol called?
funFact: Prince changed his name to an unpronounceable symbol in 1993 to protest his record label — he was referred to as 'The Artist Formerly Known as Prince' for years

questionText: Which Martin Scorsese film features 'Gimme Shelter' by The Rolling Stones?
funFact: 'Gimme Shelter' has been used in so many Scorsese films that it's practically his unofficial theme song — it appears in Goodfellas, Casino, and The Departed

questionText: Which country is Sigur Rós from?
funFact: Sigur Rós often sings in 'Hopelandic', a made-up language of nonsense syllables that sounds like Icelandic but has no actual meaning

questionText: Who is the lead singer of The Cranberries?
funFact: Dolores O'Riordan wrote 'Zombie' in one sitting after the IRA bombing in Warrington, England in 1993 that killed two children

questionText: What was Bob Dylan's birth name?
funFact: Bob Dylan won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 2016 — he didn't show up to the ceremony and took weeks to even acknowledge the award

questionText: Which film features 'Stayin' Alive' by Bee Gees in its iconic opening scene?
funFact: The Bee Gees wrote 'Stayin' Alive' in just 20 minutes — its tempo of 103 BPM is now used to teach CPR because it matches the ideal chest compression rate

questionText: Which TV show features 'Everybody Knows Your Name' as its theme?
funFact: The Cheers theme was rejected 100 times by the show's producers before the final version was accepted — it went on to become one of the most recognizable TV themes ever

questionText: Which country is Fela Kuti from?
funFact: Fela Kuti married 27 women on a single day in 1978 and was arrested over 200 times by the Nigerian government for his political activism through music

questionText: Who was the keyboardist of The Doors?
funFact: The Doors had no bass player — Ray Manzarek played the bass lines with his left hand on a keyboard bass while playing organ with his right

questionText: What nationality is Freddie Mercury by birth?
funFact: Freddie Mercury designed the Queen logo himself — incorporating the zodiac signs of all four band members: two Leos, a Cancer, and a Virgo

questionText: Which film features 'Bohemian Rhapsody' in its most famous car scene?
funFact: The Wayne's World headbanging scene sent 'Bohemian Rhapsody' back to #2 on the charts in 1992, 17 years after its original release

questionText: Which country is Rosalía from?
funFact: Rosalía studied flamenco at the Taller de Músics in Barcelona for 8 years before blending it with reggaeton and electronic music

questionText: Who was the original bassist of Pink Floyd?
funFact: Roger Waters and David Gilmour's feud was so intense that they wouldn't be in the same room — their 2005 Live 8 reunion was a genuine shock to the music world

questionText: What year did Tupac Shakur's 'All Eyez on Me' album release?
funFact: Tupac recorded 'All Eyez on Me' in just two weeks after being bailed out of prison by Suge Knight — it was the first double album in hip-hop history

questionText: Which Pixar film features 'You've Got a Friend in Me' by Randy Newman?
funFact: Randy Newman wrote 'You've Got a Friend in Me' in 15 minutes — it became the emotional cornerstone of the entire Toy Story franchise spanning 25+ years
backgroundSongName: You've Got a Friend in Me

questionText: Which Danish TV series features the song 'Forbrydelsen' composed by Frans Bak?
funFact: The Killing's atmospheric score helped make it a global phenomenon — it was remade as an American series and put Nordic noir on the world map

questionText: Which country is Gasolin' from?
funFact: Gasolin' is considered Denmark's greatest rock band ever — frontman Kim Larsen became a national treasure and his songs are sung at every Danish gathering

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Radiohead?
funFact: Radiohead released 'In Rainbows' in 2007 with a 'pay what you want' model — some paid nothing, but the average payment was higher than a typical album price

questionText: What instrument did Louis Armstrong play?
funFact: Louis Armstrong grew up so poor in New Orleans that he ate from garbage cans — he went on to become the most influential jazz musician of the 20th century

questionText: Which Western film trilogy features Ennio Morricone's 'The Ecstasy of Gold'?
correctAnswer: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
options: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
Once Upon a Time in the West
funFact: Metallica has used 'The Ecstasy of Gold' as their concert intro music since 1983 — Morricone was deeply honored by this

questionText: Which country is a-ha from?
funFact: a-ha's 'Take On Me' music video took 16 weeks to create using rotoscoping — over 3,000 frames were hand-drawn for the iconic pencil-sketch animation

questionText: Who is the vocalist of System of a Down?
funFact: All four members of System of a Down are of Armenian descent — their song 'P.L.U.C.K.' is about the Armenian Genocide, making them one of the most politically charged bands in metal

questionText: What was Johnny Cash's signature outfit color?
funFact: Johnny Cash wore black as a symbol of solidarity with the poor, hungry, and oppressed — he wrote the song 'Man in Black' to explain why

questionText: Which film features 'Danger Zone' by Kenny Loggins?
funFact: Kenny Loggins is called the 'King of the Movie Soundtrack' — he also did 'Footloose' and 'I'm Alright' from Caddyshack, dominating 1980s cinema music

questionText: Which TV series uses 'Bad Guy' by Billie Eilish in its opening credits?
funFact: While 'Bad Guy' isn't a TV theme, Billie Eilish did write 'No Time to Die' for the James Bond film at age 17 — the youngest Bond theme artist ever

questionText: Which country is Tame Impala from?
funFact: Tame Impala is essentially one person — Kevin Parker writes, records, produces, and performs almost everything himself, then hires musicians for live shows

questionText: Who is the bassist of The Beatles?
funFact: Paul McCartney is left-handed — he plays bass upside down, with the strings reversed, because he couldn't afford a left-handed bass when he started

questionText: What record label did Jay-Z found?
funFact: Jay-Z founded Roc-A-Fella Records because no label would sign him — he sold CDs from his car trunk before becoming a billionaire

questionText: Which animated film features 'Hakuna Matata'?
funFact: 'Hakuna Matata' is a real Swahili phrase meaning 'no worries' — after the film, tourism to East Africa increased partly because people wanted to experience the phrase's origin

questionText: Which country is Carlos Santana originally from?
funFact: Carlos Santana was playing guitar on the streets of Tijuana as a teenager before moving to San Francisco and becoming one of the greatest guitarists ever

questionText: Who was the lead singer of Oasis?
funFact: Liam and Noel Gallagher's feud is legendary — Noel once described Liam as 'a man with a fork in a world of soup'

questionText: What was Notorious B.I.G.'s real name?
funFact: Biggie recorded most of 'Ready to Die' while still selling drugs on the streets of Brooklyn — his mother didn't know he was a rapper until the album was released

questionText: Which film features 'Eye of the Tiger' by Survivor?
funFact: Sylvester Stallone originally wanted 'Another One Bites the Dust' by Queen for Rocky III, but Queen said no — Survivor wrote 'Eye of the Tiger' specifically for the film

questionText: Which Danish-Swedish TV series features a theme by Choir of Young Believers?
funFact: The Bridge's opening theme 'Hollow Talk' became so famous that the band went from obscure Copenhagen indie to international recognition overnight

questionText: Which country is Buena Vista Social Club from?
funFact: The musicians of Buena Vista Social Club were mostly retired and forgotten when Ry Cooder found them in Havana — their album sold over 8 million copies and won a Grammy

questionText: Who is the drummer of Rush?
funFact: Neil Peart is considered one of the greatest drummers ever — his drum kit contained over 40 pieces and his solos could last over 10 minutes

questionText: What city is Eminem from?
funFact: Eminem grew up so poor in Detroit that he changed schools 7 times — his 8 Mile Road autobiography became one of hip-hop's greatest origin stories

questionText: Which James Bond film features Adele's 'Skyfall'?
funFact: Adele recorded 'Skyfall' in just 10 minutes after reading the film's script — it won the Academy Award for Best Original Song

questionText: Which country is PSY from?
funFact: 'Gangnam Style' was the first YouTube video to reach 1 billion views — it was so popular it actually broke YouTube's view counter, forcing them to upgrade from 32-bit to 64-bit

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Pearl Jam?
funFact: Pearl Jam fought Ticketmaster in 1994 over excessive service fees — they testified before Congress and boycotted Ticketmaster venues for three years

questionText: What was David Bowie's iconic alter ego called?
funFact: David Bowie's eyes appeared to be different colors due to a teenage fist fight that left one pupil permanently dilated — it wasn't heterochromia

questionText: Which film features 'Mrs. Robinson' by Simon & Garfunkel?
funFact: 'Mrs. Robinson' was originally called 'Mrs. Roosevelt' — director Mike Nichols heard Simon playing it and said 'that's my movie', so they changed the name

questionText: Which country is Volbeat from?
funFact: Volbeat singer Michael Poulsen named the band after a volleyball move — they went from playing Danish pubs to headlining arenas worldwide

questionText: Who was the lead guitarist of Dire Straits?
funFact: Mark Knopfler plays guitar with his fingers instead of a pick — his distinctive fingerpicking style on 'Sultans of Swing' was what got Dire Straits signed

questionText: What was Whitney Houston's first number-one hit?
correctAnswer: Saving All My Love for You
options: Saving All My Love for You
funFact: Whitney Houston holds the Guinness World Record for the most consecutive number-one singles — seven in a row from 1985 to 1988

questionText: Which TV series features 'California' by Phantom Planet as its theme?
funFact: Phantom Planet was a relatively unknown band until 'California' was picked for The O.C. — the show's music supervisor discovered them at a small LA club

questionText: Which country is Laufey from?
funFact: Laufey grew up in Reykjavik playing cello in the Iceland Symphony Orchestra before becoming a jazz-pop sensation — she's bringing jazz back to Gen Z

questionText: Who is the frontman of Rage Against the Machine?
funFact: Rage Against the Machine's fans got 'Killing in the Name' to Christmas number one in the UK in 2009, beating X Factor winner Joe McElderry — a deliberate protest campaign

questionText: What year did Miles Davis release 'Kind of Blue'?
funFact: Kind of Blue was largely improvised — Miles Davis gave his musicians rough sketches just hours before recording, and most tracks were first takes

questionText: Which Sofia Coppola film features 'Just Like Honey' by The Jesus and Mary Chain in its final scene?
funFact: Bill Murray whispers something inaudible to Scarlett Johansson in the final scene — Sofia Coppola has never revealed what he said, making it cinema's most famous unheard line

questionText: Which country is Kim Larsen from?
funFact: Kim Larsen's 'Papirsklip' and 'Langebro' are played at virtually every Danish party — he's often called Denmark's most beloved musician of all time

questionText: Who was the drummer of The Who?
funFact: Keith Moon was so wild that he once drove a Rolls Royce into a Holiday Inn swimming pool — he was banned from every Holiday Inn worldwide

questionText: How many octaves could Freddie Mercury's voice span?
funFact: Scientists studied Freddie Mercury's voice posthumously and found he used subharmonics — a technique usually seen in Tibetan throat singing, not rock music
